feedback.text: Please consider including video games that the consumer has paid for, but the publishing company decides that the customer does not own the game bought, but only a license to use it for an unspecified amount of time. Therefore, companies can render a game unplayable according to their whim. Some companies (UBISOFT) even include a clause in the EULA stating that the consumer must uninstall and potentially destroy all copies of the game when the company ceases support or the game's "life cycle" ends.
